title: String.isEmpty() should be used to test for emptiness
description: Using String.isEmpty() is the preferred and most readable way to check if a string is empty. Using alternative approaches, such as comparing to "" or checking length, can be less clear and more error-prone.
recommendation: Replace manual string emptiness checks with String.isEmpty().
impact: Improves code readability and reduces the risk of subtle bugs when checking for empty strings.

checks.message: Use String.isEmpty() to test for string emptiness instead of other methods.
